name: New Pearl Harbor
description: The 'New Pearl Harbor' theory posits that the U.S. government either orchestrated or knowingly allowed the September 11, 2001, attacks to occur, using them as a pretext for military intervention in the Middle East and the expansion of governmental powers. This theory draws a parallel to the attack on Pearl Harbor, which was used to justify U.S. entry into World War II. Proponents argue that the official narrative contains inconsistencies and that evidence suggests prior knowledge or deliberate inaction by government officials. Key arguments include alleged foreknowledge of the attacks, suspicious trading activity before 9/11, anomalies in the collapse of the World Trade Center towers, and perceived failures in the response by the military and intelligence agencies. Mainstream perspectives reject these claims, citing extensive investigations, scientific analyses of the collapse, and the absence of credible evidence supporting government involvement. Critics argue that the theory relies on misinterpretations of data, speculation, and a misunderstanding of the complexities involved in intelligence gathering and national security. The 9/11 Commission Report, while acknowledging failures in communication and coordination, explicitly refuted claims of government complicity. The theory continues to circulate, fueled by distrust of government and a desire to understand the complex events of 9/11.
subtitle: Alleged government complicity in 9/11.

claims.claim: The U.S. government had prior knowledge of the 9/11 attacks.
claims.evidence: Alleged intelligence warnings ignored, drills simulating similar attacks on 9/11.
claims.criticism: Intelligence warnings were vague and non-specific; drills are routine and do not indicate foreknowledge.

claims.claim: The collapse of the World Trade Center towers was a controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: Evidence of molten metal, symmetrical collapse, and alleged presence of explosives.
claims.criticism: Molten metal was likely from fires; symmetrical collapse explained by structural damage; no credible evidence of explosives found.

claims.claim: The U.S. military deliberately stood down, allowing the attacks to occur.
claims.evidence: Delayed response by fighter jets, alleged orders preventing interception.
claims.criticism: Response time was consistent with standard procedures; no evidence of intentional delays or stand-down orders.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Author and prominent proponent of the theory

keyFigures.name: Richard Gage
keyFigures.role: Architect advocating for controlled demolition theory

mythologyConnections: The idea of a powerful, secretive cabal controlling events

timeline.event: Continued circulation and debate of the 'New Pearl Harbor' theory

sources.author: David Ray Griffin
